title: Select the execution profile for add-execution-profiles
context: The risk x execution-profile model decouples assurance (light/standard/strict) from ceremony (micro/compact/full). This change is prompt-layer only and touches no runtime code, but it still runs under the standard risk level. Choosing the execution profile determines how many sessions, decision stops, and reports the lifecycle will spend. Pick the profile that matches the change's blast radius.
recommendation: compact: the change is small and prompt-only, so full ceremony is not warranted, but the standard risk level still requires the full gate set.

options.label: micro
options.description: Single session, minimal stops, one summary-first report. For trivial, low-risk edits.
options.consequence: Fastest path, but batches decisions aggressively and may under-document a change that later needs an audit trail.
options.risk: Too light for a change that gates downstream work.

options.label: compact
options.description: Consolidated sessions, one consolidated report, batched independent decisions, full gate set retained.
options.consequence: Keeps full assurance while removing repeated ceremony; roughly two sessions and four stops for a small change.
options.risk: None material for a prompt-only change.

options.label: full
options.description: Every phase in its own session with a per-phase report. For high blast-radius or strict-risk changes.
options.consequence: Maximum auditability at maximum token and interaction cost.
options.risk: Ceremony overhead disproportionate to a prompt-only change.
